items.title: India probe finds Tata Steel, JSW Steel, SAIL breached antitrust law, regulatory order shows: report
items.description:
JSW declined to comment, while Tata Steel, SAIL, and the executives did not respond to Reuters queries.
Reuters reported in 2022 that the watchdog raided some small ⁠steel companies as part of an investigation into the industry.
JSW Steel has 17.5% of the Indian market, Tata Steel 13.3% and SAIL ‍10%, according to data from commodities consultancy BigMint.
In the last fiscal year to March 2025, JSW Steel reported standalone revenues of $14.2 billion, while Tata Steel’s were $14.7 billion.
At 8:52 a.m. GMT, shares in JSW Steel extended losses to 1.33%, SAIL was down 3.2%, and Tata Steel turned negative and fell as much as 0.7%.

items.title: CREDAI to organise three-day property show in Vijayawada from January 9
items.description:
The real estate sector in Andhra Pradesh has witnessed a revival following the State government’s measures to ensure the availability of sand at affordable prices and simplify building regulations, said CREDAI Vijayawada Chapter chairman C. Sathish Babu.
Later, CREDAI Vijayawada Chapter president G. S. R. Mohan Rao announced that the 11th property show would be held on January 9, 10 and 11 at A Convention Centre in the city, and that Mr. Naidu had been invited to inaugurate the event.
The three-day property show will feature 50 stalls, and prizes worth ₹1 lakh in the form of gold and diamond ornaments will be given away daily through a lucky draw.
Prospective buyers will have the opportunity to purchase properties directly from CREDAI member builders, he said.
A number of banks, builders and infrastructure companies will participate in the event.

items.title: Leak at Andhra Pradesh natural gas well partially contained, say officials
items.description:
The blowout at the natural gas well owned by the Oil and Natural Gas Corporation (ONGC-Rajahmundry Asset) on the outskirts of Irusumanda village in Dr. B. R. Ambedkar Konaseema district of Andhra Pradesh has been contained by about 90%, officials said on Tuesday (January 6, 2026) evening.
A massive fire, with flames leaping up to a height of 25 metres, broke out on Monday following a gas leak at the Mori-5 well.
The incident occurred during workover activity intended to enhance the flow of natural gas.
Mr. Saxena stated that the safety management personnel would immediately contain the natural gas flow and deploy equipment into the well before killing it for further assessment of its condition.
ONGC Rajahmundry Asset Executive Director Santanu Das has said that the loss of natural gas is yet to be assessed.

items.title: Lebanon says Israeli strikes undermine efforts to prevent escalation
items.description:
Lebanese President Joseph Aoun on Tuesday (January 6, 2026) condemned Israel's latest strikes on his country, saying they undermine efforts to prevent escalation.
Mr. Aoun's criticism came after Israel launched a series of strikes in southern and eastern Lebanon on Monday (January 5, 2026), saying it hit Hezbollah and Hamas targets.
In spite of a year-old ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah, Israel has carried out frequent strikes on Lebanon, usually saying it is bombing Hezbollah sites and operatives, and occasionally Hamas targets.
On Tuesday, the Israeli military said its operations the day before struck infrastructure "belonging to the Hezbollah and Hamas terrorist organisations in several areas in Lebanon".
The latest strikes come with the committee monitoring the ceasefire, which includes the United States, France, Lebanon, Israel and the United Nations, set to meet on Wednesday.

items.title: Prevention key to tackling human trafficking, says official
items.description:
She was speaking at a regional-level Training of Trainers programme on Prevention of Human Trafficking and Girl Child Safety, organised by the Women Development and Child Welfare Department in Vijayawada.
She said District Mission Coordinators had been appointed as nodal officers in every district to ensure effective coordination of schemes.
She said emergency assistance was available through the toll-free number 112 and One-Stop Centres to ensure the safety of women and children.
Emphasising that prevention rather than post-incident response was the government’s core approach, she said efforts were under way to make the State a model in child safety and women empowerment.
Social activist Sunitha Krishnan spoke about the distressing conditions faced by victims of human trafficking.

items.title: Multi-level parking planned for hilltop temples, says AP Endowments Commissioner
items.description:
Endowments Commissioner K. Ramachandra Mohan said that measures are being taken to provide better amenities to pilgrims at all major temples across the State.
Speaking to the media, Mr. Ramachandra Mohan said that Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) that had been formulated is being implemented at major temples across the State to ensure 100% satisfaction for pilgrims.
He announced that plans are in process to provide multi-level parking at hilltop locations including the Simhachalam temple and at the Sri Durga Malleswara Swamy temple in Vijayawada to ease parking woes.
He directed temple officials to ensure to keep to the quality of ‘laddu’ and other ‘prasadams’ made in the temple kitchens.
The Commissioner also directed the officials to ensure proper management of queue lines, sanitation and supply of safe drinking water.

items.title: Water leaks following partial damage to Hippargi barrage gate
items.description:
Engineers found that a plate on the 22nd gate of the dam was damaged, probably due to the pressure of impounded water.
The dam now has around 6 tmcft of water.
Around 0.72 tmcft of water is estimated to have leaked, though the exact quantum is yet to be calculated.
Mr. Thimmapur instructed Deputy Commissioner Sangappa and engineers to consult experts and ensure that the damage is repaired properly.
He said that there is no report of crop damage due to the leakage.

items.title: Yemen separatists set for Riyadh talks after fighting: official
items.description:
The leader of Yemen's separatists is heading to Riyadh on Tuesday (January 6, 2026) for talks, a Yemeni official told AFP, after days of fighting between his UAE-backed forces and pro-Saudi rivals.
The Saudi-organised talks indicate the beginning of de-escalation efforts after the clashes in Yemen's government-run areas that exposed a rift between Gulf powers Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates.
Mr. Alzubidi will also "discuss the southern dialogue sponsored by Riyadh", the official said.
Saudi Arabia had called for talks between the Yemeni factions in Riyadh at the request of Yemen's government, with both camps agreeing to take part.
In December, the UAE-backed separatists seized large swathes of territory, including much of Hadramawt province bordering Saudi Arabia, expelling Saudi-backed government forces.

items.title: State to act firmly against hate speech and misinformation on social media
items.description:
Andhra Pradesh Education, IT and Electronics Minister Nara Lokesh on Tuesday asserted that the State government would act firmly against individuals and groups spreading malicious, hate-filled and obscene content on social media platforms.
The meeting discussed key legal and regulatory provisions, including the IT Act, 2000, the IT Rules, 2021, the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, and judicial safeguards related to free speech and privacy.
Emphasising the urgency of curbing AI-generated deepfake and obscene content, the Minister suggested framing age-based regulations for social media usage.
Mr. Lokesh observed that organised and malicious campaigns were being run on social media to provoke controversy, often spreading widely within seconds.
Calling for a comprehensive study of social media regulatory frameworks elsewhere, Mr. Lokesh cited examples from Australia, the European Union and the United Kingdom, where independent regulatory mechanisms with heavy penalties are in place to deter misuse of digital platforms.

items.title: KPRS demands higher MSP for red gram, 50% import duty on pulses
items.description:
Members of the Karnataka Prantha Raitha Sangha (KPRS) staged a protest outside the Agricultural Produce Market Committee (APMC) office in Kalaburagi on Tuesday, demanding an increase in minimum support price (MSP) for red gram crop.
The farmers demanded that the Union government increase MSP to ₹12,500 per quintal of red gram.
He urged the Centre to impose a 50% import duty on pulses to protect domestic farmers.
The protesters sought the implementation of the Swaminathan Commission recommendations for all farm produce and demanded the establishment of red gram procurement centres to ensure assured procurement.
The farmers also urged the State government to immediately open red gram procurement centres across the district and ensure the early release of pending crop insurance claims.

items.title: China bans exports to Japan of dual-use goods that could enhance Tokyo's military power
items.description:
The Chinese Commerce Ministry said in a statement that exports of such items, which can serve both civilian and military purposes, to Japanese military users and all other end-users that could help enhance Japanese military power are prohibited.
Relations between Japan and China worsened late last year after Japan's Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi said its military could get involved if China were to take action against Taiwan.
In December, Japan said Chinese military aircraft locked radar on its fighter jets even though there was a safe distance between them.
Last week, China launched large-scale military drills around Taiwan for two days to warn against what it calls separatist and external forces.
Japan was defeated in World War II by the United States and its allies.

items.title: S. Chandran of Merryland Studio passes away
items.description:
S. Chandran, director of City Theatres and son of P. Subramaniam, founder of Merryland Studio, passed away here on Tuesday.
After the passing of Subramaniam, Mr. Chandran had taken over the management of the studio and the running of the cinemas Sreekumar, Sree Visakh, New, and Sree Padmanabha, which are some of the oldest surviving movie houses in the capital.
In a post on X, actor Kamal Haasan wrote - “I was deeply saddened to hear of the passing of Mr. Chandran, the son of Merryland Subramaniam.
He was an ardent admirer of art, with a special interest in photography.”S. N. Raghuchandran Nair and Abraham Thomas, president and secretary of Trivandrum Chamber of Commerce and Industry respectively, condoled the death of Mr. Chandran.
Thereafter, the body will be taken to the Sree Padmanabha Theatre and then to the New Theatre buildings in Thampanoor.

items.title: Men’s HIL: Dragons notch up second straight win
items.description:
CHENNAI: When it comes to set-plays and sheer pace in transition, Tamil Nadu Dragons is second to none.
Just two minutes later, Dragons doubled their advantage through forward Uttam Singh, who slid in to push the ball into the net.
A long ball by birthday boy Harmanpreet Singh resulted in Dragons defender Sander de Wijn fumbling inside the circle.
But the Dragons’ defence held firm to see the match through and condemn Soorma to its second successive defeat.
The result: Tamil Nadu Dragons 3 (Selvaraj 28, Uttam 30, Govers 47) bt JSW Soorma 2 (Maninder 45, Harmanpreet 58-pc).

items.title: If we don't win midterms, I will get impeached: Trump to House Republicans
items.description:
U.S. ​President Donald Trump said ‌on Tuesday ​(January 6, 2026) that Republicans must win the 2026 midterm elections or else he will get impeached ​by Democrats.
“You gotta ⁠win the midterms ‘cause, if we don’t win ​the midterms, ⁠it’s just gonna be — I mean, they’ll find a ‌reason to impeach ‌me,” Mr. Trump said at a ‍retreat for House Republican lawmakers in ‍Washington.
“I’ll get impeached.”Mr. Trump’s agenda is on the line in November’s elections, when all the seats in ⁠the House of Representatives and ​a third of the Senate’s ⁠seats will be contested.

items.title: Rethinking India’s skilling outcomes | Explained
items.description:
Between 2015 and 2025, India’s flagship skilling programme, Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana, has trained and certified around 1.40 crore candidates.
India’s Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) stands at 28%, but the National Education Policy 2020 aims to raise it to 50% by 2035.
The most serious structural failure in India’s skilling ecosystem lies with the Sector Skill Councils (SSCs).
SSCs were meant to play this role at a national scale.
India’s skilling challenge is a failure of accountability, not of intent or government funding.

items.title: LIC introduces new plan Jeevan Utsav Single Premium
items.description:
The Life Insurance Corporation (LIC) of India has launched Jeevan Utsav Single Premium, a non-par, non-linked, individual, savings, whole life insurance plan.
It is a single premium plan with accrual of guaranteed additions during the guaranteed additions period.
The Unique Identification Number (UIN) for LIC’s Jeevan Utsav Single Premium Plan is 512N392V01.
The plan gives guaranteed life long income and life long risk, and is available for ages starting from 30 days to 65 years.
LIC’s Jeevan Utsav single Premium will be available for purchase from January 12, 2026.

items.title: U.S. Justice Dept says millions of Epstein files still not released
items.description:
The department began releasing documents from the decades-long investigation into the late disgraced financier last month, but failed to meet the December 19 deadline mandated under the Epstein Files Transparency Act.
In a letter on Monday to a federal judge, DOJ officials said more than two million documents remained "in various phases of review."
The DOJ said it identified on December 24 more than one million files not included in its initial review.
At least 100 FBI employees trained in handling "sensitive victim information" will assist the effort.
In Monday's letter, the DOJ officials said they must "manually" review the documents for "victim identifying information."

items.title: Chief Minister to visit Polavaram project on January 7
items.description:
Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu will visit the Polavaram irrigation project on Wednesday, January 7, to personally review the progress of works at the State’s prestigious multi-purpose project.
During the visit, Mr. Naidu will inspect key components of the project, including the Polavaram viewpoint, the coffer dam, the buttress dam and the works underway at ECRF Gap-1 and Gap-2.
Officials said the Chief Minister is keen on closely monitoring the quality and pace of works, given the strategic importance of the project to Andhra Pradesh.
Officials said the Polavaram project, described as the lifeline of the State, has gained significant momentum in recent months.
Over the last 18 months, works have accelerated considerably, with nearly 13% of construction completed during this period alone.

items.title: Demand to ban non-Hindus from Ardh Kumbh Mela gains momentum
items.description:
A day after the Shri Ganga Sabha, which manages religious activities at various ghats of Haridwar, demanded a ban on the entry of non-Hindus into Ardh-Kumbh mela premises, religious leader Sadhvi Prachi also made a similar demand on Tuesday.
The government is expecting at least 6-7 crore visitors to the fair, which will end on Maha Shivratri.
Speaking to the media, Sadhvi Prachi cited the Red Fort car blast case in New Delhi and sought a ban on the entry of non-Hindus into the fair area.
A day earlier, Shri Ganga Sabha president Nitin Gautam told The Hindu that the organisation had urged the government to declare the fair area ‘exclusive’ for Hindus.
“It is the need of the hour that, ahead of the upcoming Kumbh, the entry of non-Hindus should be banned at all Ganga ghats and religious places in the Kumbh Mela area,” he said.

items.title: PM Modi ‘not that happy with me’ because they are paying a lot of tariffs: Trump
items.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump on Tuesday (January 6, 2026) said that Prime Minister Narendra Modi is "not that happy with me" because of the tariffs imposed by Washington on Delhi for its purchases of Russian oil.
Mr. Trump, delivering remarks at the House GOP Member Retreat, also claimed that “Prime Minister Modi came to see me, ‘Sir, may I see you please’.
He's not that happy with me because you know they're paying a lot of tariffs now because they're not doing the oil, but they are, they've now reduced it very substantially, as you know, from Russia,” Mr. Trump said.
Mr. Trump has imposed 50% tariffs on India, including 25% for its purchases of Russian oil.
Mr. Trump also said that India told him it has been waiting for five years for the Apache helicopters.

items.title: Madras High Court Chief Justice keeps mandatory e-filing requirement in abeyance
items.description:
Madras High Court Chief Justice Manindra Mohan Shrivastava on Tuesday ordered indefinite postponement of the mandatory requirement of filing cases only through the electronic mode in the district courts located both in Tamil Nadu and Puducherry.
An official notification issued by the High Court’s Registrar General S. Alli stated that the Chief Justice had issued directions to the Registry, on the administrative side of the court, to keep the mandatory e-filing requirement in abeyance until further orders.
The High Court’s Registrar General had initially issued a notification on September 24, 2025 for implementing mandatory e-filing of cases in all district courts from October 8, 2025.
However, acceding to a request from the Bar associations, the Chief Justice had postponed the date of implementation to December 1, 2025.
Meanwhile, a joint action committee of FBATNP also decided to lay siege to the High Court campus on Wednesday by gathering advocates from all districts.

items.title: Bengaluru traffic police to crack down on high-beam, coloured lights from today
items.description:
Two-wheeler and four-wheeler riders in the city have been cautioned against using high-intensity lights and unauthorised additional lamps, as the Bengaluru Traffic Police will launch a special enforcement drive from January 7 to 13.
According to police, several motorists have been fitting vehicles with red, yellow and blue coloured lights and excessively bright headlights, causing inconvenience to other road users and increasing the risk of accidents.
and causing nuisance especially during night time.
To curb the menace and improve road safety, Joint Commissioner of Police (Traffic), Bengaluru, Karthik Reddy has directed traffic police to conduct strict checks, register cases against violators and impose penalties during the drive.

items.title: BJP, Congress brace for showdown in Punjab over new rural jobs scheme
items.description:
The BJP, according to its State president Sunil Jakhar, intends to “expose the misleading propaganda being spread by the Aam Aadmi Party and the Congress against this welfare scheme for the poor”.
With the implementation of this scheme, corruption will be curbed, and the entire wage amount will directly reach the labourers’ bank accounts.
This is precisely the reason why the AAP government and the Congress are opposing this new pro-poor scheme and spreading falsehoods about it in society,” added Mr. Jakhar.
The Congress, on the other hand, has termed the BJP’s campaign last-minute ‘fire fighting’ measure against the Congress’s nation-wide campaign, ‘MGNREGA Bachao Sangram’.
Congress State unit president Amarinder Singh Raja Warring said, “The BJP was trying to defend the indefensible as it was worried about the party’s nation-wide campaign.”

items.title: Indian farmers fear a flood of cheap New Zealand apples if duty cuts take effect
items.description:
“Indian farmers rely on controlled-atmosphere cold storage to sell apples during the off-season, when they earn prices that sustain their families for the entire year.
Traders fear that the agreement, which proposes to reduce the import duty from 50% to 25%, could hit apples stored for the off-season trade hard.
In Kashmir, 15 lakh families are associated with the apple trade, and it generates ₹30,000 crore annually.
Flood the marketThis is a common refrain amongst apple growers in Himachal Pradesh too, which produces apples worth ₹5,000-6,000 crore annually and engages over 1.5 lakh families.
Ravinder Chauhan, president of the Apple Growers Association of India, said that the Centre’s proposed move to slash import duty on New Zealand apples “will flood the market with cheaper apples”.

items.title: Temporary nurse relieved from duty
items.description:
R. Ranjitha, serving as a temporary nursing staff under the Chief Ministers’ Health Insurance Scheme at the Thanjavur Medical College was relieved from her post by the Dean on Tuesday.
According to sources, TMC Dean Poovathi received complaints from cancer ward patients and their attendants at the Super Speciality Wing of the hospital stating that a nurse ill-treated patients.
Subsequently, a video clip of the nurse directing patients to come to her seat and administering IV fluids to them was forwarded to the Dean and was also uploaded on social media.
After conducting an inquiry, the Dean issued termination order to her on January 6, the sources added.

items.title: Garbage blackspots return in southeast Bengaluru as collection vehicles arrive late
items.description:
Parts of southeast Bengaluru, including Adugodi, Ejipura, and Koramangala, are again witnessing a rise in garbage blackspots, as waste collection vehicles are reportedly arriving two to three hours late.
Bengaluru Solid Waste Management Limited (BSWML) had earlier stated that fines were being collected only in localities where garbage collection vehicles were operating regularly.
“The timings of the waste collection vehicle are unpredictable, as they differ every day and are often delayed by more than an hour.
While irregular timings remain a concern in Koramangala, several localities across Ejipura are struggling to dispose of garbage as waste collection vehicles reportedly arrive late every day.
Locals have also alleged that the waste collection kiosk installed in the area around three months ago remains non-operational for most of the day.

items.title: Maiden Kochi Corporation council meeting witnesses heated political debate over PMAY-U project
items.description:
The first resolution moved at the maiden meeting of the newly elected Kochi Corporation Council, seeking to relax the lock-in period of title deeds of houses built under the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana – Urban (PMAY-U) before they are released to beneficiaries, descended into a heated political debate on Tuesday.
The resolution was moved by UDF councillor K.X.
“The Centre’s insistence on differentiating houses built under PMAY with a colour code, thereby branding beneficiaries as poor and dependent on government aid, is disgraceful.
Keralites, and no government in Kerala, will accept that,” he said, drawing staunch protests from BJP councillors.
Minimol said that the resolution may be considered passed with a request to implement PMAY-U 2.0 and to relax the lock-in period.

items.title: BJP seeks probe into land conversions under previous BRS regime
items.description:
BJP Legislature Party leader Alleti Maheshwar Reddy on Tuesday (January 06) demanded that the State government conduct a detailed probe into the alleged irregularities in land conversions during the previous BRS regime.
Stating that the policy would remain voluntary, Mr. Reddy countered Opposition criticism that it should be mandatory.
Responding to allegations that 9,000 acres and ₹5 lakh crore were siphoned off, Mr. Reddy described the criticism as unsubstantiated.
Participating in the discussion, Minister Jupally Krishna Rao explained that HILT policy was not a sudden decision.
Mr. Rao said Chief Minister Revanth Reddy constituted a Cabinet Sub-Committee on pollution control and resource mobilisation.

items.title: MP holds discussions with BIAL officials on new parking rules
items.description:
Following a representation by taxi drivers’ associations, Dr. Sudhakar convened a meeting with representatives of the drivers and airport authorities.
He pointed out that while app-based aggregators charge fares in the range of ₹1,200–1,500, local private taxi drivers often offer services at significantly lower rates.
Addressing concerns raised by airport officials about congestion, he suggested that parking space could be made available to private taxi drivers by levying a nominal user fee, ensuring orderly access without overcrowding.
He added that 18,000 to 20,000 youth from Chickballapur, Bengaluru Rural, Kolar, Tumakuru districts, and neighbouring regions earn their livelihood through taxi services at the airport.
The MP said, if necessary, the issue would be taken up with Civil Aviation Minister K. Ram Mohan Naidu for further deliberation.

items.title: Patient with rare cardiac complication treated at Aster Ramesh Hospitals
items.description:
A 37-year-old man from Bhimavaram in West Godavari district, who suffered severe cardiogenic shock due to hyperthyroidism, has recovered after treatment at Aster Ramesh Hospitals.
Doctors placed him on veno-arterial ECMO, which allowed the heart to rest while recovery therapy was administered.
After four days, his heart function improved from 15% to about 50%, following which he was weaned off all support.
He is now stable and ready for discharge, said P. Ramesh Babu, Managing Director and Chief Cardiologist.

items.title: KRMB urges Andhra Pradesh and Telangana to release funds due from 2024-25
items.description:
HYDERABADThe Krishna River Management Board (KRMB) has requested the member State Andhra Pradesh and Telangana to release funds against the approved budget of ₹23.32 crore for 2025-26 and 2024-25 since it has been facing acute financial crisis even in meeting the salary expenditure of the board secretariat.
In a letter addressed jointly to the Engineers-in-Chief (Irrigation/Water Resources) of the two States, the river board has pointed out that neither of the two States have released any funds for the past three quarters (April to December 2025).
As a result, staff salaries were being paid by temporary diversion of available funds of ₹4.15 crore of Telangana meant for the installation of second-phase telemetry equipment.
For the second-phase, 14 locations were agreed for by the member States and additional 15 locations were proposed by the river board.
Another 22 locations were also proposed by Telangana.

items.title: UDD issues final notification amending setback rules for Bengaluru buildings
items.description:
The Karnataka Urban Development Department (UDD) on Monday issued a final notification amending setback norms for buildings built on plots of up to 4,000 sq.
The final notification introduces staggered setbacks for sites up to 250 sq.
m (20X30 sites) have to leave a setback of 0.75 m in the front and 0.6 m on any one side.
For plots above 250 sq.
The front setback must be 12% of the site depth, the rear setback 8% of the site depth, and the side setbacks 8% of the site width on both sides.

items.title: CIAL handles over 10 million passengers for third consecutive year
items.description:
Cochin International Airport Limited (CIAL) has handled over 10 million passengers for the third consecutive year in 2025, according to the company.
During the period between January and December 2025, CIAL handled a total of 1,15,19,356 passengers, registering a year-on-year growth of approximately 4.85% over 2024, when it handled recorded 1,09,86,296 passengers, it said.
May emerged the busiest month of the year, with passenger traffic crossing 11.07 lakh, closely followed by January and December with 10.44 lakh and 10.06 lakh respectively.
However, aircraft movements registered a minor dip to 74,689 in 2025 compared to 75,074 in 2024.
CIAL will shortly introduce full-body scanners for strengthening security screening standards while reducing physical checks, including frisking, and enhancing passenger comfort, the communication said.

items.title: Political leaders condole death of V.K. Ebrahim Kunju
items.description:
Political leaders condoled the passing away of V.K.
Ebrahim Kunju, former Minister and Indian Union Muslim League (IUML) leader, here on Tuesday.
He had immense experience as evident from his transformation from a student leader to an administrator and political leader, he said.
Ramesh Chennithala, senior Congress leader, said that he had made major contributions to the Kerala society as former Minister for Industries and Public Works.
Hibi Eden, MP, said in a communication that Mr. Kunju had made several contributions to the development of Ernakulam.

items.title: Watch: Ancient labyrinth discovered in Maharashtra’s Solapur region
items.description: Skiers form giant heart in snow to honour victims of Swiss resort tragedyBy

items.title: As BDA turns 50, DKS urges institutional reform, end of corruption
items.description:
Shivakumar on Tuesday called for a fundamental transformation of the Bangalore Development Authority (BDA), urging officials to shed its long-standing image associated with “brokerage” and “unethical” practices.
Mr. Shivakumar revealed that it took him six months to understand the internal functioning of the Authority, which is plagued with corruption.
Mr. Shivakumar pointed to plans to start a town planning college in Bengaluru through Visvesvaraya Technological University (VTU).
Calling BDA and the Greater Bengaluru Authority (GBA) the “face of Bengaluru”, Mr. Shivakumar said citizens must approach the authorities with trust and dignity.
We must ensure our contribution helps build a better Bengaluru,” he said.

items.title: Rising attacks on Hindus in Bangladesh deeply worrying: Assam CM
items.description:
GUWAHATI:Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma said the rising attacks on minority Hindus in Bangladesh were a matter of deep concern and called for extending moral support to the community in the neighbouring country.
“What is happening in Bangladesh is deeply worrying for us.
As the incidents of attacks on the Hindus may have their effects in Assam, we will have to remain cautious and monitor the situation,” he told journalists on Tuesday (January 6, 2026).
Protests in ParisConcerns over the attacks on minorities in Muslim-majority Bangladesh were also raised in Paris.
Demanding an end to all forms of atrocities on Hindus and other minorities in Bangladesh, BHRJ president Dipan Mitra said that targeting Hindus by accusing them of blasphemy and occupying Hindu monasteries and temples has become routine in Bangladesh.

items.title: Sonia Gandhi admitted to Delhi's Ganga Ram Hospital; condition stable
items.description:
Congress leader Sonia Gandhi has been admitted to Sir Ganga Ram Hospital in New Delhi after she suffered respiratory discomfort, sources said on Tuesday (January 6, 2026).
At present, she is doing fine, and her condition is "absolutely stable", they said.
It is a routine admission, but she has this problem of chronic cough, and she keeps coming for check-ups, especially with this pollution in the city, a hospital source told PTI, adding that she was admitted on Monday (January 5) evening.
She had complained of some respiratory discomfort, and upon medical examination, it was found that "her bronchial asthma had been mildly exacerbated" due to the combined effects of cold weather and pollution, the source said.
As a precautionary measure, she was admitted for further observation and treatment.

items.title: Mahindra introduces XUV 3XO electric starting ₹ 13.89 lakh
items.description:
Mahindra & Mahindra Ltd. (M&M) on Tuesday launched battery powered XUV 3XO EV starting at ₹13.89 lakh (ex showroom).
The XUV 3XO EV perfectly balances urbane design, performance, safety and cutting-edge technology, the company said.
“The XUV 3XO EV builds on this foundation for customers who typically use the vehicle for intra-city commutes and want an electric SUV that feels like a smart next step,” it added.
The XUV 3XO EV has been engineered around real usage patterns, ensuring that it meets everyday mobility needs reliably.
It combines instant electric performance with a package that is tuned for confidence and ease in city driving,” he added.

items.title: VTU to act on private companies demanding money from students instead of providing paid-internships
items.description:
There have been allegations that many private companies registered on the Visvesvaraya Technological University (VTU) portal are demanding money from students for internships, instead of providing paid internships.
More than 2,500 companies that have agreed to provide paid internships to students of VTU-affiliated colleges have registered.
However, there have been allegations that some private companies, instead of providing paid internships to students, are demanding money from the students.
It is not right to allow companies that charge money from students to register on VTU portal,” alleged a student of a private engineering college.
Therefore, a meeting of large private companies will be called and a request will be made to provide more internships to VTU students.

items.title: Changes under MGNREGA condemned
items.description:
Members of the Tamil Nadu Association for the Rights of All Types of Differently Abled and Caregivers (TARATDAC) staged a demonstration outside the Collectorate on Tuesday, condemning recent changes to the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA).
A. Balamurugan, district secretary of TARATDAC, alleged that under the new Viksit Bharat - Guarantee Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) (VB-GRAM G), several protections mandated by the original MGNREGA had been dismantled.
“While MGNREGA mandated the inclusion of a specific percentage of differently abled individuals in every work batch, the VB-GRAM G framework lacks such inclusive norms,” Balamurugan stated.
He noted that under previous rules, differently abled workers were eligible for full daily wages for four hours of labour.
He said that while all rural workers are at risk, the differently abled community would bear the most immediate and devastating impact of these job losses.

items.title: General Upendra Dwivedi highlights modern warfare, defence ties during UAE visit
items.description:
In his address, General Dwivedi highlighted the evolving global security landscape and the changing character of modern conflicts, underscoring the growing importance of technology in contemporary warfare.
Training convergenceAs part of the visit, General Dwivedi also interacted with Major General Staff Yousef Maayouf Saeed Al Hallami, Commander, UAE Land Forces.
In another key engagement, General Dwivedi met the Ambassador of India to the UAE, Deepak Mittal.
Following the visit to the UAE, Gen. Dwivedi will visit Sri Lanka from January 7-8.
General Dwivedi will also pay homage at the Indian Peace Keeping Force (IPKF) War Memorial, honouring the supreme sacrifice of Indian soldiers.

items.title: Man arrested for disrupting traffic, public nuisance on Avinashi Road in Coimbatore
items.description:
The police on Tuesday arrested a Kerala native on charge of disrupting traffic, causing public nuisance and preventing police personnel from discharging their job on arterial Avinashi Road late on Monday, January 5, 2026.
As per a release issued by the Coimbatore City Police, the incident took place around 10.30 p.m. on Monday.
It said that the man caused fear among the public and attempted to injure them with his vehicle, besides threatening them.
The police rescued the man, who suffered minor injuries, and admitted him to Coimbatore Medical College Hospital.
The Peelamedu police registered a case against him on Tuesday for offences such as criminal intimidation, assault or criminal force to deter public servant from discharge of his duty, wrongful restraint, public nuisance and rash driving, based on which he was arrested.

items.title: Basketball Nationals | Delhi gets past Punjab in a topsy-turvy contest
items.description:
In the women’s section, Railways, Karnataka and Kerala booked a berth in the last eight after winning their third league game.
Jayalakshmi 17, R. Sreekala 16) bt West Bengal 48 (Madhu Kumari 28).
Santosh 16) bt Gujarat 68 (Sahaj Kumar Patel 17, Krishnapalsinh Gohil 16, Vinay Kaushik 16, Jigrajsinh Gohil 13).
Group B: Indian Railways 94 (Amrendra Nayak 15, Vishnu Prasad 15, Manik 14, Surya Pratap Singh 12) bt Chandigarh 43 (Sanju Gajbhiye 10).
Delhi 79 (Jeethandar Singh 21, Ayush Sharma 12, Issac T. Thomas 11, Abhishek Tyagi 11, Rachit 10) bt Punjab 62 (Tajinderbir Singh 20).

items.title: Congress high command backs demand for share in power in T.N., say TNCC leaders
items.description:
Our All-India Congress Committee (AICC) in-charge Girish Chodankar posed ‘which political party will say no to power?’.
Asked if the Congress leaders in New Delhi could step in to resolve the impasse as it has happened in the past, he said, “I don’t know what has happened in the past.
Alagiri said the Congress high command also recognised the need for share in power even as he recognised the close relationship that Congress’ leaders Sonia Gandhi and Rahul Gandhi shared with Mr. Stalin.
“One month ago, AICC in-charge Girish Chodankarji communicated the party’s demands on share in power and the number of seats.
However, a share in power is necessary for the Congress to grow in Tamil Nadu,” he said.

items.title: Frequent parole to Gurmeet Singh is ‘very disappointing’, says son of slain journalist; seeks court intervention
items.description:
Gurmeet Singh was convicted in the murder case of Ramchander Chhatrapati in 2019.
In a video following the release of the Dera chief on 40-day parole a day ago, the journalist’s son Anshul Chhatrapati described the frequent grant of parole and furlough to Gurmeet Singh as “very disappointing”.
Gurmeet Singh was granted frequent parole citing his good conduct inside the jail, but for someone convicted in a rape and murder case, their conduct outside the jail held more significance, he argued.
He said that he suspected the political and financial considerations behind the frequent release of the Dera chief.
He said that not only Gurmeet Singh, but 6,000 other criminals too were entitled to similar temporary release.

items.title: T.N. govt. notifies SOP for large public gatherings
items.description:
The Tamil Nadu government has released a detailed Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) for the regulation and management of public gatherings in the State on the directions of the Madras High Court.
However, contrary to a proposal in an earlier draft SOP, the State did not mandate collection of security deposits from organisers of rallies and meetings in the final notification.
Prior concurrence from the concerned authorities is required if the venue is adjacent to National Highways, State Highways or any other public road.
For events scheduled at notified designated venues, applications must be submitted at least 10 days in advance, but not earlier than 21 days.
For events proposed at alternative, non-notified venues, applications must be submitted at least 15 days in advance, but not earlier than 30 days.

items.title: For civic polls, Congress-led Mumbai Vikas Aghadi promises to stop privatisation of hospitals
items.description:
A day after the Mumbai Congress released the manifesto to rebuild the BEST services, the Aghadi released manifesto, showcasing their vision on public services, including healthcare, education, climate, infrastructure, and basic facility of water supply and waste management.
Mumbai Congress president Varsha Gaikwad released the manifesto on Tuesday, saying the party would improve the services of water supply, sanitation, road, and waste management.
Under health, the party aims to run OPD services, MRI, and CT-scan services full time.
For hawkers, the Congress plans to implement street vendor Act, 2014 and rehabilitate eligible hawkers in the hawker zone.
To treat the poor air quality and climate issues, the Congress has plans to introduce “Green Mumbai 2030”, that will expand the green cover in Mumbai.

items.title: One more held for Walayar lynching
items.description:
The police have arrested one more person in connection with the mob lynching of Ram Narayan Baghel, 31, a labourer from Chhattisgarh, at Attappallam, near Walayar.
The special investigation team (SIT), which is investigating the alleged xenophobic lynching that took place on December 17 afternoon, arrested Rajesh, 39, from East Attappallam.
Eight others were already arrested in connection with the incident which had shaken the State.
Rajesh, a headload worker at Walayar, was an accused in five criminal cases, said the police.
The police said Rajesh was among the group that mercilessly thrashed Ram Narayan accusing him of theft.

items.title: NC leaders oppose separate Statehood for Jammu, push for ‘reunification of Ladakh’
items.description:
Meanwhile, senior NC leader and Minister Javed Rana called for the “reunification of Ladakh and J&K”.
Mr. Sharma’s statement did not reflect ground realities, Mr. Rana said.
“There has been no demand for a separate Jammu State from the Pir Panjal Valley and the Chenab Valley.
The people in these valleys continue to stand with the idea of a unified Jammu and Kashmir,” Mr. Rana said.
Mr. Abdullah met Mohd Jaffer Akhoon, Chairman and Chief Executive Councillor of the Ladakh Autonomous Hill Development Council-Kargil.

items.title: School Health Programme to be launched
items.description:
The Health and Education departments will jointly launch a comprehensive School Health Programme in the State, said Health Minister Veena George here on Tuesday.
Children will be issued individual health cards as part of the programme to systematically monitor and assess their health status, Ms. George, said.
She was speaking after inaugurating the State-level observance of National Deworming Day here on Tuesday.
January 6 is observed as National Deworming Day.
The deworming tablets are to be administered to children strictly in accordance with the instructions of health workers.

items.title: ‘Science and technology must help improve everyday life’
items.description:
Sharanappa has said that science and technology, through new innovations, should help improve the mindset of people and make everyday life better for the common man.
Speaking after inaugurating the 42nd anniversary celebrations of the District Science Centre in Kalaburagi on Tuesday, he said that the Science Centre provides an excellent platform for students to develop scientific awareness alongside formal education.
District Science Centre officer K.M.
Sunil spoke about the importance of the Science Centre in nurturing scientific curiosity among students.
The former District Science Officer Lakshmi Lakshminarayana and a large number of school students were present.

items.title: NTR laid strong foundation for Rayalaseema irrigation projects, says Minister Ramanaidu
items.description:
Water Resources Minister Nimmala Ramanaidu on Tuesday said that Rayalaseema’s transformation from a drought-prone region to a productive agricultural zone was rooted in the vision of former Chief Minister N.T.
Mr. Naidu later expanded these initiatives and worked consistently to realise the irrigation potential of the region.
The Minister said focused attention by the present coalition government had improved water management, with reservoirs and tanks across Rayalaseema showing healthy storage levels.
Mr. Ramanaidu said the Handri–Neeva project, considered Rayalaseema’s lifeline, had received major funding under the current government, unlike the previous five years.
He maintained that the present government was focusing on strengthening existing infrastructure, ensuring safety, and improving long-term water availability for Rayalaseema’s farmers.

items.title: Three arrested with 1.4 kg ganja in Dhoolpet
items.description:
Three individuals were arrested and 1.412 kilograms of dry ganja was seized during a raid conducted by the State Task Force team in coordination with officials of the Telangana Prohibition and Excise Department in Dhoolpet.
The raid was carried out following a tip-off about the illegal possession and sale of ganja from a residential premises at Imlibagh, Lower Dhoolpet.
Those arrested have been identified as Aarthi Bai, Goutam Singh alias Golu and Sabita Bai, all residents of Dhoolpet.
Three others, identified as Aavish Singh, Sarika and Kailash Singh, are absconding, police said.
The seized ganja and mobile phones were taken into custody, and further investigation is under way to trace the absconding accused and identify the source of the narcotics.

items.title: Cyberabad police urge parents to reduce academic pressure on students after a tragic suicide in Gachibowli
items.description:
The Cyberabad police issued an advisory appealing to parents, teachers and students to treat examinations as only a part of life.
This advisory follows the death of a school student who ended his life in Gachibowli allegedly after being asked by his parents to get ready for school.
Referring to the incident, police said increasing academic pressure and constant comparison over marks and ranks are pushing students into extreme mental stress, with some feeling that there is no future beyond examinations.
Police likened life to sport, where both victories and defeats are inevitable, and encouraged students to bounce back from failures.
The advisory called on parents to recognise that their children’s lives and well-being are far more important than marks or ranks, warning that excessive pressure in the name of academic achievement can be counterproductive.

items.title: National boxing: Lovlina edges Saweety; Panghal survives Priyanshu’s challenge
items.description:
Former World championship silver medallist Saweety Boora faced off against Olympic bronze medallist Lovlina Borgohain in the second round of the 75kg category at the National boxing championships here.
It took Lovlina by surprise and the opening round duly went to the elder pro from Haryana.
Important results: Women: 51kg: Nitu Ghanghas (Har) bt Priya (HP) RSC; 54kg: Sakshi Chaudhary (SSCB) bt Manasa Veni Anakapalli (AP) RSC.
; 75kg: Lovlina Borgohain (Asm) bt Saweety Boora (Har) 3-2.
Men: 55kg: Jadumani Singh (SSCB) bt R. Parthiban (TN) RSC; Amit Panghal (SSCB) bt Priyanshu (Har) 3-2; 60kg: Hussamuddin (SSCB) bt Ashish (HP) 5-0.

items.title: Man arrested for murdering a woman near Kazhugumalai
items.description:
The police have arrested a youth for allegedly murdering his teenaged girl friend near Kazhugumalai on Monday for ending her relationship with him.
In a bid to pacify Uma, Rajesh took her to nearby Kasthurirengapuram on Monday on his motorcycle.
Upset by this, Rajesh allegedly strangulated her.
As Uma swooned, Rajesh called the ambulance over the phone and alerted the Vembakottai police, who rushed to the spot and found Uma dead.
The police arrested Rajesh and sent Uma’s body for post-mortem.

items.title: Union Minister Dharmendra Pradhan releases 55 literary works developed by classical language centres
items.description:
Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan on Tuesday (January 6, 2026) officially released 55 volumes of literary works in classical Indian languages like Kannada, Odia, Telugu, Malayalam, and Tamil, along with a sign-language series of the Tirukkural by Tamil poet Thiruvalluvar.
A government statement said that of these literary works, 41 books were developed by the Centres of Excellence for Classical Languages under the Central Institute of Indian Languages (CIIL).
In addition, 13 books and the sign language series of Tirukkural have been brought out by the Central Institute of Classical Tamil.
Mr. Pradhan also posted independent messages on social media hailing the launch of these books in their respective languages.
In another post about the works released in Odia, Mr. Pradhan said this was a “proud moment for the world of Odia literature and culture”.

items.title: Belagavi Kala Mandir to be leased out
items.description:
The Belagavi district administration plans to lease out the day-to-day management of the Kala Mandir cultural complex in the city.
This follows an instruction from Deputy Commissioner Mohammad Roshan who held a meeting of Smart City Limited officers recently.
Engineers of Smart City Limited built the centre at a cost of ₹47.8 crore.
However, art lovers have urged the State government to start the centre as soon as possible.
Managing Director, Belagavi Smart City Limited, Kavita Warangal said that a fresh tender will be called soon.

items.title: Bengaluru Open: Dhakshineswar packs off Ajdukovic; Karan, Aryan bow out
items.description:
Dhakshineswar Suresh came up with a power-packed performance to oust Croatia’s Duje Ajdukovic 6-4, 6-4 in the opening round of the Dafa News Bengaluru Open ATP Challenger 125 at the S.M.
It did not matter that Ajdukovic was nearly 200 ranking places better off (329 to Dhakshineswar’s 521), and was a three-time ATP Challenger titlist.
The second, in fact, was to love, and the easy volley that Ajdukovic dumped into the net on break-point was emblematic of his struggles.
But his countrymen Karan Singh and Aryan Shah didn’t fare well as they tamely bowed out.
Other results (first round): Singles: Cedrik-Marcel Stebe (Ger) bt Max Houkes (Ned) 4-6, 6-3, 6-1; Felix Balshaw (Fra) bt Benjamin Hassan (Lbn) 6-4, 6-1; Borna Gojo (Cro) bt Karan Singh 6-2, 6-4; Eric Vanshelboim (Ukr) bt Niels Visker (Ned) 3-6, 6-2, 6-4; Beibit Zhukayev (Kaz) bt Aryan Shah 6-1, 6-2; Mitsuki Wei Kang Leong (Mas) bt Aziz Ouakaa (Tun) 6-2, 6-2; Pedro Martinez (Esp) bt Eero Vasa (Fin) 4-6, 6-2, 6-3.

items.title: Study Qatar model for Sports City development in Amaravati, says Chandrababu Naidu
items.description:
Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu has directed officials to study the Qatar model for developing a world-class Ssports City in Amaravati, positioning the capital as a hub for international-standard sports infrastructure.
The directive was issued during the 57th CRDA meeting, chaired by the Chief Minister on Tuesday.
Emphasising sustainable revenue generation, the Chief Minister instructed theofficials to financially strengthen the Amaravati Development Corporation (ADCL) and the AP CRDA by adopting robust revenue models and creating income-generating assets.
He reiterated the vision of transforming Amaravati into a Blue-Green City, with internal canals, riverfront beautification, and sustainable water management.
The Chief Minister directed officials to ensure no inconvenience to the farmers.

items.title: Govt. first-grade colleges set a target of 75% admissions this year
items.description:
With an aim to increase enrollment in government first-grade colleges across the State, the Department of Collegiate Education (DCE) is all set to conduct a special admission drive for 2026-27, Praveshati Abhiyana, in all government pre-university colleges in the State.
As part of the campaign, the principals of government first-grade colleges have been advised to visit all government pre-university colleges by January 20 and introduce and convince students about the courses available in their colleges and the job opportunities available from them, thereby increasing the number of admissions in degree colleges.
All the first-grade colleges have been directed to form an admission committee for the purpose.
Meanwhile, it has come to the notice of the Department that faculties in some colleges are making duplicate admissions to facilitate their work.
The admission drive is being held in the backdrop of dwindling admissions in government first-grade colleges due to a lack of necessary infrastructure, students being attracted to private colleges and universities, among a host of other reasons.

items.title: Piyush Goyal to visit Brussels in ‘decisive push’ to conclude India-EU FTA talks
items.description:
“Union Minister of Commerce and Industry, Shri Piyush Goyal, is scheduled to visit Brussels for a significant two-day official trip from 8-9 January 2026,” the Ministry of Commerce and Industry said in a release.
“The visit underscores the intensifying diplomatic and technical engagements between New Delhi and Brussels, signalling a decisive push toward concluding the India-EU Free Trade Agreement (FTA).”Although no official announcement has yet been made, it is expected that the Summit will see an announcement on the conclusion of the India-EU FTA negotiations.
During his visit, Mr. Goyal will hold high-level dialogues with the European Union’s Commissioner for Trade and Economic Security Maroš Šefčovič.
“India is pushing for zero-duty access for its labour-intensive sectors — such as textiles, leather, apparel, gems and jewellery, and handicrafts,” the release said.
“Both India and the EU have expressed strong political resolve to deliver a comprehensive deal.”

items.title: Man found murdered near Seevalaperi
items.description:
A man was found murdered near Seevalaperi on Tuesday.
The police said a 30-year-old man was found murdered near Seevalaperi cremation ground on Tuesday.
After the passers-by alerted the Seevalaperi police, the body was sent for a post-mortem.
During investigation, the police found that Martin, 30, of Mudhalur near Sattankulam in Thoothukudi district, who had come to his relatives’ house in Seevalaperi, had been murdered after he consumed liquor with a few persons on Monday night.

items.title: 17-year-old Mumbai boy arrested while transporting ganja via Hyderabad
items.description:
A 17-year-old boy being used as an inter-State drug courier was arrested during a joint operation by Telangana Elite Action Group for Drug Law Enforcement (EAGLE) Force and the Hyderabad police on Monday.
The officials seized 10 kilograms of ganja worth about ₹5 lakh during a joint operation in the city.
Police said that the boy, a tempo cleaner from Bhiwandi in Maharashtra, was exploited by a Mumbai-based drug syndicate that targeted his vulnerable financial background.
Investigation revealed that the main accused, identified as Hazi Shafiullah Shaik, lured the juvenile in the last week of December 2025 with an offer of ₹1,500 and asked him to travel to Odisha on the pretext of transporting a vehicle.
The three accused, Mehfuz, Hazi Shafiullah Shaik and Khala, all residents of Mumbai, are currently absconding, and efforts are under way to arrest them.

items.title: Deputy CM Bhatti calls for productivity shock to achieve a $3-trillion economy by 2047
items.description:
He had presented the government’s ambitious Telangana Rising 2047 Vision document during a short discussion in the Legislative Assembly on Tuesday.
“Our objective is to place every citizen on the path of progress,” he asserted, adding that Self Help Groups (SHGs) will be elevated into corporate entities to drive women-led growth.
The Deputy CM noted that Telangana’s Gross State Domestic Product (GSDP) has historically doubled every decade since 1991, making it a $200-billion economy today.
Citing the German model, Mr. Bhatti Vikramarka stressed that “buildings do not build nations, but people do”.
On women-led growth, the Finance Minister announced plans to make one crore women millionaires, not through subsidies but enterprise.

items.title: Health summit to be held
items.description:
Global Preventive ONCO Summit (GPOS) 2026 will be held from January 16 to 18 at hotel Hyatt Regency in Thiruvananthapuram.
The three-day continuing medical education (CME) programme is expected to bring together leading oncologists, public health experts, researchers, policymakers, and health care professionals from across India and abroad to deliberate on strategies aimed at reducing the global cancer burden.
The summit is being organised under the leadership of M.V.
Pillai, chairperson of the GPOS 2026, with Mohanan Kunnummal serving as the general convener and Satheeshan Balasubramanian as the working chairperson.
The event seeks to build on the success of the Cancer Safe Kerala initiative by strengthening preventive oncology through early detection, community-based screening, and evidence-driven public health interventions.

items.title: Doctors across Telangana to hold black badge protest on January 7 against G.O. 229
items.description:
Doctors across Telangana will begin a Statewide black badge protest from Wednesday, in opposition to Government Order Ms. No.
229, raising concerns that the move threatens the autonomy and democratic functioning of the Telangana Medical Council (TGMC).
As part of the protest, doctors will wear black badges while continuing their regular duties, with the associations stating clearly that patient care will not be affected in any manner.
The protest has been announced by the Medical Joint Action Committee (JAC), which comprises the Indian Medical Association (IMA)-Telangana State, Healthcare Reforms Doctors Association, Telangana Junior Doctors Association, Telangana Senior Residents Doctors Association and the Telangana Teaching Government Doctors Association.
229 and called for adherence to the High Court’s emphasis on maintaining a decisive majority of elected members in medical councils.

items.title: Flour mill owner abducted, found murdered near Courtallam
items.description:
The Courtallam police have arrested three persons, including a 17-year-old boy, for allegedly murdering a flour mill owner.
The police said Ramanathan alias Ramkumar of Veeravanallur near Cheranmahadevi in Tirunelveli district, owner of a flour mill, who was renting out furniture, had enmity with broiler shop owner Selva Manikandan of the same area after the former refused to give tables and chairs to the later.
As Ramkumar’s mobile phone remained switched off for a few hours, his wife Usha Rani filed a complaint with the Veeravanallur police.
During investigation, the police found that Ramanathan had been abducted to a cottage in Courtallam where he was found murdered.
The Courtallam police have registered a case and arrested Selva Manikandan, Gowtham, and a 17-year-old boy.

items.title: TNPCB to organise mass e-waste collection drive
items.description:
Tamil Nadu Pollution Control Board (TNPCB), along with Madurai district administration in collaboration with E Cycle Solutions Private Limited, Chennai, is to organise a mass e-waste collection drive at MADITSSIA Hall between 10 a.m. and 5 p.m. in Madurai on January 8.
A statement from the organisers said the mission of the initiative was to engage every sector of the society to responsibly dispose the electronic wastes.
The collected e-waste would be sent to authorised waste management agency for safe and scientific recycling or disposal.
With current trend in increasing generation of e-waste, improper disposal poses a significant threat to the environment, mainly due to toxic substances used in electronic items such as lead, cadmium, mercury and brominated flame retardants, which affects the quality of environment and human health, if not disposed of scientifically.
Hence, the organisers, requested the public, residential establishments, educational institutions, private organizations, commercial establishments, industries, government departments, local bodies and so on, to join this initiative by handing over their electronic wastes during the event.

items.title: Due to high speed winds, fishermen advised not to enter sea for fishing
items.description:
Due to high-speed sea winds, fishermen in Rameswaram and Mandapam have been prohibited from venturing into the sea from Tuesday.
Additionally, sea waves are expected to rise to a height of three meters.
Consequently, fishermen have been advised to stay off the Palk Strait.
The Fisheries Department has further instructed fishermen not to venture out until further notice and to ensure the safety of their fishing boats and equipment.
As a result, tokens for entering the sea would not be issued on Wednesday at the fishing harbours in the district, including Rameswaram, Mandapam, Devipattinam, Thondi, and S.P.

items.title: Actor Mohanlal to be ‘goodwill ambassador’ of KSRTC
items.description:
Actor Mohanlal will act as the ‘goodwill ambassador’ of the Kerala State Road Transport Corporation (KSRTC), Transport Minister K.B.
Addressing a press conference here on Tuesday, he said the actor has agreed to be the goodwill ambassador of the public utility without charging any payment.
The corporation is planning to shoot a few advertisements featuring Mohanlal for the KSRTC, making use of services of talented directors, the Minister said.
In September 2025, the KSRTC had netted a record collection of ₹10.19 crore, which was revised in December 2025 with a collection of ₹10.76 crore.
The Minister stated that the record collection is the result of making minor changes in the operations of the buses.

items.title: CPI(M) leader’s attack on Binoy Viswam sparks sharp rebuttal from CPI
items.description:
He made the remarks while addressing a public meeting at Mannoor, near here, on Tuesday, where longstanding tensions between the CPI(M) and CPI have persisted.
He accused Mr. Viswam of behaving like a petty politician and alleged that the CPI had become a party that claims credit whenever it secures a victory.
In a response to the criticism, CPI Palakkad district secretary Sumalatha Mohandas said that Mr. Ajaya Kumar was mentally imbalanced.
Ms. Mohandas said Mr. Viswam is nationally well known as a communist party leader.
Ms. Mohandas also pointed out that Mr. Ajaya Kumar had been elected as an MP twice with the support of CPI votes.

items.title: Pongal event attended by Amit Shah religiously divisive: CPI State secretary
items.description:
CPI State secretary, M. Veerapandian on Tuesday described the recent Pongal celebration organised by the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) and attended by Union Home Minister Amit Shah as a “religiously divisive event”.
Addressing reporters in Mayiladuthurai, Mr. Veerapandian said Tamilians had always upheld unity and social harmony and cautioned against the use of cultural symbols to deepen religious divisions.
He alleged that Mr. Shah selectively participated in Tamil cultural events for electoral gains, while recalling that the Union Minister had earlier made derogatory remarks about Tamils in Odisha without expressing regret.
On State politics, he said the CPI had the political legitimacy to seek additional seats in the forthcoming Assembly elections.
He urged the government to address issues faced by unorganised workers and suggested including turmeric in the Pongal gift hamper.

items.title: Karur textile units scale down weekly operation due to poor orders from the U.S.
items.description:
The textile units in Karur usually close for a day on Sundays, thereby workers getting one day weekly off.
However, the dwindling orders from the U.S.A. have forced the exporters to run their units only for five days or four days a week.
But, the poor export order, mainly from the U.S.A., has forced us to scale down the production.
Some companies operate five days in a week, others prefer to operate from Monday to Thursday,” says P. Gopalakrishnan, president, Karur Textile Manufacturers Association.
The exporters had hardly received fresh orders from the US in the second and third quarters of the current fiscal.

items.title: Bannerughatta Biological Park gains from safari bans in other popular destinations
items.description:
The ban on safari at popular destinations Kabini, Nagarhole, and Bandipur has benefitted the Bannerughatta Biological Park (BBP), which saw record numbers during the recently concluded holiday season.
According to data shared by Surya Sen A.V., Deputy Conservator of Forests and executive director, Bannerughatta Biological Park, the days between December 20, 2025, and January 5, 2026, saw record footfall and safari visitors.
This period witnessed 1,95,763 total footfall, of which 1,06,215 were safari visitors and 89,548 visitors to the zoo.
During the same period the years before (2023-2024 and 2022-2023), these numbers stood at 1,66,330 footfall, 79,754 zoo visitors and 86,576 safari users, and 1,60,590 footfall, 89,283 zoo visitors and 71,307 safari users respectively.
The park resumed safari operations after a temporary suspension in the backdrop of a second leopard attack during a safari in a matter of four months.

items.title: Naukuchiatal: A nine-cornered escape into the Kumaon Hills
items.description:
A few extra bends ahead, a third, lesser-thought-of lake town beckoned, as if it had been waiting for the road to empty out first.
High in these Kumaon foothills, nestled at around 4,000 ft above sea level, Naukuchiatal or the “nine-cornered lake”, came into view.
It was easy to see why wellness in this valley felt native.
After months of breathing in Delhi’s noxious fumes, the simple act of standing by Naukuchiatal felt like a balm.
In the indescribable calm of around this furtive lake town, I think I might just have discovered something close to what Cooper had been fawning over in Twin Peaks after all.

items.title: Hotel receptionist dies in road accident
items.description:
A 23-year-old man was killed in a road accident near Wipro Circle in Gachibowli in the early hours of Tuesday after the motorcycle he was riding rammed into a road divider.
The deceased was identified as Suraj Singh, a native of Uttar Pradesh, who was working as a receptionist at a hotel in Gowlidoddi, Serilingampally.
Police said they received a call through Dial 100 at about 1.46 a.m. about the accident.
According to the preliminary enquiry, Singh lost control of his motorcycle and hit the divider near the main gate of Virtusa on the road towards Nanakramguda.
A case has been booked and further investigation is under way.

items.title: Eluru Forest Division gets 13 vehicles under CAMPA scheme
items.description:
The Eluru Forest Division has acquired 13 new vehicles under the Compensatory Afforestation Fund Management and Planning Authority scheme to strengthen forest protection and conservation efforts.
The vehicles have been procured for both the Territorial and Wildlife wings of the division and will be used for forest protection, wildlife conservation, fire prevention and curbing illegal tree felling.
Officials said the initiative forms part of the State government’s broader efforts to conserve forest resources, promote greenery and maintain ecological balance with active public participation.
The utilisation of CAMPA funds for acquiring essential infrastructure, they noted, underlines the priority being given to strengthening forest protection mechanisms.
The new fleet is expected to play a key role in improving forest management, ensuring quicker response during emergencies and enhancing supervision of conservation programmes across the division, according to a press release.

items.title: Adikavi Nannaya University earns top certification for academic excellence, green initiatives
items.description:
Information Technology and Human Resources Development Minister Nara Lokesh has lauded Adikavi Nannaya University Vice-Chancellor S. Prasanna Sree’s efforts to obtain a five-star rank in academic excellence, curriculum implementation, faculty performance, green initiatives and sustainable practices, and achieving 100% results in B. Ed colleges.
Recently, HyM International Certifications Private Limited inspected the university campus and assessed the overall performance and standards of the university.
In an official release on Tuesday, Prof. Prasanna Sree stated that Mr. Lokesh was briefed about the academic and green initiatives on the university campus during the meeting of the Vice-Chancellors held on January 5.

items.title: Officials hold mock polling to test EVMs and VVPATs in Thoothukudi
items.description:
Mock polling was conducted at the District Collectorate on Tuesday to test the electronic voting machines, control units, and the Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail (VVPAT) to be used in the forthcoming Assembly elections after these machines underwent first-level checking.
As the first level checking of the EVMs and the VVPATs commenced on December 11, the service engineers from Bharat Electronics Ltd., which supplied the poll gadgets, were checking 4,575 EVMs, 2,423 control units, and 2,648 VVPATs to be used in the upcoming Assembly election this year.
Of this, 15 EVMs, 25 control units, and 59 VVPATs were found to be faulty during the first level checking and hence remaining 9,548 polling gadgets have been kept ready for the polling.
After the first level checking was completed, mock polling was conducted at the Collectorate on Tuesday.
District Collector K. Elambahavath and the representatives of recognised political parties were present to validate the smooth functioning of the EVMs, control units and the VVPATs.

items.title: Vedupari ritual held at Srirangam temple
items.description:
Devotees in good numbers witnessed the ‘Thirumangaimannan Vedupari’, a holy ritual held at the Sri Ranganathaswamy Temple at Srirangam on Tuesday as part of the Vaikunta Ekadasi festival.
The ritual was held on the eighth day of the ‘Raa Pathu’.
The ritual is held to mark the blessings that the Lord conferred on Thirumangaimannan when he robbed Lord Narayana and His consort, disguised as a newly wed couple, of their valuables.
According to legend, the Lord revealed himself and initiated Thirumangaimannan into the ‘Ashtakshara Mantra’.
The episode is enacted every year as part of the ‘Vaikunta Ekadasi’ festival at the Srirangam temple.

items.title: Centenary celebrations of CPI: Kavi Sammelan to be held on January 12 in Khammam
items.description:
A ‘kavi sammelan’ will be held in Khammam on January 12 as a prelude to the concluding meeting of the CPI’s centenary year celebrations slated to be held in the town on January 18.
According to party sources, the literary event will be organised at the DPRC Bhavan in the town.
A galaxy of eminent literary personalities, including progressive poets will participate in the event.
An anthology of poems on the centenary of the CPI will be released at the event.
Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy and Communist Party of India Secretary D. Raja, among others, will address the public meeting to be held in Khammam on January 18, party sources added.

items.title: State task force calls for anti-larval drive, study on dog bite deaths
items.description:
She is a member of a State-level task force committee constituted by the government to strengthen measures for the prevention and control of communicable diseases.
The 24-member task force, headed by Health and Family Welfare Commissioner G. Veerapandian, was constituted following instructions issued by Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu and includes national experts and officials from various departments.
The first meeting of the task force was held virtually on Tuesday evening, during which experts offered suggestions on steps to prevent infectious diseases in the State.
Concluding the virtual meeting, Mr. Veerapandian said efforts would be made to implement the action points discussed at the first task force meeting within four weeks.
Officials, including Director of Public Health Padmavathi, participated in the meeting.

items.title: Assignment of Kerala University land to AKG Centre challenged before HC
items.description:
A retired Joint Registrar of Kerala University has approached the Kerala High Court, challenging the alleged assignment of 15 cents of land belonging to Kerala University to the Communist Party of India (Marxist)’s former State headquarters AKG Centre (the AKG Memorial Research Centre) in Thiruvananthapuram.
It was nothing but land grabbing by use of muscle power of the political party.
Despite repeated representations and applications under the Right to Information Act, the competent authorities had failed to provide the impugned GO.
The alleged assignment was illegal, unconstitutional, and void, being in violation of the Kerala University Act and of principles governing public property, he submitted.
The matter was forwarded to the Bench of the High Court Chief Justice, which hears public interest litigations.

items.title: It is employer’s responsibility to ensure the driver possesses valid licence: Karnataka High Court
items.description:
A total of 11 persons, including seven children, had died in the accident.
AppealThe head master of Balmy Primary School, Thenkulipady, Mangaluru taluk in Dakshina Kannada district, had filed an appeal against the award passed by the civil court in 2015 in favour of Mariyamma, wife of the deceased driver, Badruddin, and her two minor children.
The insurance company, Bajaj Allianz General Insurance, had declined to pay compensation under the WC Act while contending that Badruddin was driving the bus, owned by the school, on the date of the accident, August 14, 2008, even though his driving licence expired on June 17, 2008.
The insurance company had claimed that the compensation under the WC Act had to be paid by the employee as the school’s head master, in whose name the bus was registered, was negligent in not ensuring the renewal of driving licence of the driver and hence violated the condition of insurance.
Apex court orderThe insurance company had relied on the 2020 judgement of the apex court, in the case of Beli Ram vs Rajendra Kumar and others, which had said that ensuring that the driver possesses a valid driving licence and it is renewed timely is part of the care to be taken by the employer to protect the interest of his employee, the driver under the Workmen Compensation Act.

items.title: Tiger found dead in Bhadra Tiger Reserve
items.description:
A tiger, aged about eight to ten years, was found dead at Gangegiri (Chikkamagluru taluk) in Hebbe Range of the Bhadra Tiger Reserve on Tuesday.
The preliminary investigation by the officials suggested infighting as the cause of the death.
A team of forest personnel engaged in the All-Tiger Estimation-2026, an exercise to assess the tiger population, noticed the carcass of the tiger and informed higher officers.
Pulkit Meena, DCF of Bhadra Tiger Reserve, told The Hindu that the preliminary investigation suggested that the death could be due to infighting.
However, the officials had collected the samples for further examination.

items.title: Owner of a two-wheeler with fuel leakage in railway parking area arrested
items.description:
The owner of a two-wheeler parked at the Wadakkanchery railway station was arrested and the vehicle seized after a petrol leak was detected during safety monitoring of the station’s parking area on Tuesday, railway authorities said.
According to a railway press release, Railway Protection Force (RPF) personnel rushed to the spot immediately after receiving information about the suspected leak.
The RPF has registered a case under Section 154 against the vehicle owner.
The two-wheeler was seized and the owner arrested in accordance with rules, the release said.
A detailed fire safety audit of parking areas at railway stations is being carried out jointly by the railways’ commercial and electrical departments to enhance safety and prevent recurrence of such incidents.

items.title: T.N. Cabinet clears TAPS; Stalin to launch ‘Ungal Kanavai Sollungal’ household survey
items.description:
The Tamil Nadu Cabinet, chaired by Chief Minister M.K.
Stalin, on Tuesday cleared the new Tamil Nadu Assured Pension Scheme (TAPS) for State government employees, which guarantees an assured pension equal to 50% of the last-drawn basic pay.
The Chief Minister will launch the survey on January 9 from Ponneri in Tiruvallur district and it will cover 1.91 crore families across Tamil Nadu.
The State government also plans to consult entrepreneurs of Tamil Nadu origin residing in other countries on January 12, observed as the Day of Non-Resident Tamils.
Individuals who have contributed to district development, along with MLAs, MPs and other elected representatives, will be consulted to gather district-specific development projects.

items.title: Béla Tarr dies at 70: a storied legacy of slowing cinema down
items.description:
Tarr later distanced himself from this period, but its material concerns never disappeared.
From this point onward, Tarr abandoned psychological motivation and conventional plotting in favour of spatial choreography and temporal pressure.
Tarr’s later films maintained the same formal approach established in the 1990s.
Shortly after its release, Tarr announced his retirement from feature filmmaking, stating explicitly that repetition held no interest for him.
Jim Jarmusch’s later films have similarly adopted extended observation and deadpan pacing associated with Tarr’s work.

items.title: Elections to standing committees in progress in Kozhikode Corporation
items.description:
The election of members to the eight standing committees of the Kozhikode Corporation was held at the Corporation office on Tuesday (January 6) and will continue on Wednesday (January 7).
Elections to the development, health, works and welfare standing committees were held on Tuesday.
Elections to the standing committees are unusual in the Kozhikode Corporation, and members used to be chosen through a political understanding.
Elections to the remaining standing committees — finance, education and sports, tax and appeal, and town planning- will be held on Wednesday.
Elections to the posts of chairpersons of the standing committees will be held within five days of the election of the members.

items.title: ED files supplementary chargesheet in Sahiti Infratec money laundering case
items.description:
The Directorate of Enforcement (ED) has filed a supplementary prosecution complaint before a special court in Hyderabad against senior executives of Sahiti Infratec Ventures India Pvt Ltd in connection with the real estate fraud and money laundering case involving hundreds of home-buyers.
The supplementary complaint was filed under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act, 2002, against B. Lakshminarayana and Sandhu Purnachandra Rao, former director and sales and marketing head of the company.
Despite collecting money, the company allegedly failed to deliver flats or refund the amounts, thereby cheating investors of their savings.
As the probe widened, multiple FIRs were registered based on complaints from buyers across different projects undertaken by Sahiti Infratec and its group entities.
The ED also found that Sandhu Purnachandra Rao allegedly misappropriated around ₹126 crore from the company, including over ₹50 crore collected in cash.

items.title: Lottery held for 111 Telangana Housing Board LIG flats in Gachibowli
items.description:
The lottery for the allotment of 111 Telangana Housing Board (THB) flats in Gachibowli for Lower Income Groups (LIG) was held on Tuesday (January 6, 2025) after a prolonged delay.
The lottery was held in the presence of a few applicants in the hall and the process was streamed live on YouTube.
Delay flares tempers at lottery venueEarlier on Monday, the Board had appealed to the people not to visit Nirman Kendra — the lottery venue — in Gachibowli and had urged them to watch the process online.
They huddled and questioned authorities about the reason for the delay and demanded explanation on the lottery process.
Gautham, said that thorough scrutiny of applications led to the delay, but the flats were allotted in a transparent manner.
